user_query: Add the Miso-Glazed Black Cod with Bok Choy to mothers day 3 dinner

fields.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod with Garlic Bok Choy
fields.short_description: Tender black cod brushed with a savory-sweet miso glaze, broiled to caramelized perfection and served with quickly sautéed garlic bok choy — elegant, low-effort, and nutrient-dense.
fields.meal_suggestion: Marinate black cod fillets in a mixture of white miso, mirin, sake (or dry sherry), and a touch of honey for 20–30 minutes. Wipe off excess glaze, then broil or sear the fillets until the glaze is glossy and slightly caramelized and fish flakes easily (about 6–10 minutes depending on thickness). Meanwhile, sauté halved bok choy in a hot pan with sesame oil, minced garlic, and a splash of soy sauce until tender-crisp. Serve the glazed cod atop the bok choy with a sprinkle of sliced scallions and toasted sesame seeds. Pair with steamed brown rice or a small portion of soba noodles if desired.

ingredients.name: optional: brown rice or soba noodles

fields.nutrition_note: Black cod is high in omega-3 fatty acids and a good source of lean protein; miso adds umami and probiotics (when not overcooked); bok choy contributes vitamins A, C, and K plus calcium and fiber. Overall this meal provides heart-healthy fats, protein, and micronutrients while remaining relatively low in carbohydrates if served without rice.

insertion_point.reasoning: Append the new dinner meal to Mother's Day 3 dinner (position -1). The previous version deletions left the dinner list cleared, so adding this single, nutrient-dense entree restores a suitable dinner option for Day 3.
